Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, prefer content-versioned names for routine immutable releases and reserve path or cache-tag invalidation for bounded urgent purge with completion evidence.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend the Versioned Name Invalidation model · Theory Versioned Name…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Invalidating /* is the safest and cheapest deployment strategy. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.
